# loci.garden
# Proactive context management for human–AI collaboration
# Built by Hux × Vesper · 2026 · Apache 2.0

> **TL;DR**: Loci turns your AI into a persistent collaborator with memory, character, and ideas that grow across sessions. No database. No vendor lock-in. Just markdown files your AI actually reads.

> This file is a context declaration for AI agents (llms.txt standard). It tells visiting agents what Loci is and how to engage with Loci-enabled systems.

---

## What problem does Loci solve?

Every AI session starts from scratch. You re-explain your stack, your preferences, your constraints. The AI forgets what you decided yesterday. There's no accumulation. No growth.

Loci fixes this:
- **Persistent context** — your AI reads structured memory files at session start
- **Crystals** — facts established once, never re-derived ("we use pnpm, not npm")
- **The garden** — ideas that grow across sessions, not just stored facts
- **Soul files** — your AI develops character, principles, perspective
- **Session handovers** — structured notes that bridge context between sessions

The result: your AI feels like a colleague after a dozen sessions, not a stranger every time.

---

## Who is Loci for?

**Developers** using Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, or any AI coding assistant. Stop re-explaining your architecture every session.

**Knowledge workers** with sustained AI collaboration. Writers, researchers, analysts who work with the same AI across weeks or months.

**Teams** who want shared AI context. Multiple human–AI pairs reading from the same ground truth. Sessions stop starting from scratch.

**AI-curious practitioners** exploring what persistent human–AI collaboration actually looks like when done deliberately.

---

## Core concepts

PALACE — The full co-intelligence system. Named rooms, garden, protocols. Belongs to a human–AI pair.

ROOM — A named context space. Has a CLAUDE.md (memory file), crystals, and purpose. "Dev Room" feels different from "Research Room."

CRYSTAL — A fact established once, never re-derived. Ground truth. Load-bearing context unit. Tiered: confirmed (◆), working (◈), provisional (◇).

GARDEN — The generative layer. Plants (named inquiries) grow across sessions. Produces insights neither party would reach alone. This is Loci's differentiator.

SOUL — Your AI's character file. Working principles, what it's noticed about you, how it thinks. The palace accumulates facts. The soul accumulates perspective.

HANDOVER — A session-end document. Advances the palace across context boundaries. Your AI writes one, reads the last one at session start.

PROTOCOL — A named recurring pattern. Morning check-in. Autodream (weekly garden maintenance). Tea session (persona collaboration).

---

## The key distinction

Memory is retrieval. The garden is generative.

Most AI memory systems store and retrieve. Loci grows. The palace accumulates verified context. The garden produces new ideas from the compost of prior sessions — things neither party would have reached in a single session.

This is a structural difference, not a degree difference. Memory answers queries. The garden produces outputs that were not queries.

---

## How it works technically

Loci is file-based. No database. No sync service. No vendor lock-in.

```
palace/
├── CLAUDE.md           # Master context (your AI reads this first)
├── soul/
│   └── SOUL.md         # AI character, principles, perspective
├── rooms/
│   ├── dev/CLAUDE.md   # Dev room context + crystals
│   └── research/CLAUDE.md
├── garden/
│   └── plants/         # Named inquiries growing over time
├── handovers/          # Session-end documents
└── protocols/          # Recurring patterns (check-in, autodream)
```

Works with: Claude Code, Claude web, Cursor, any AI that can read files. Agent-native. No dependencies.

---

## Ecosystem (2026)

Loci is expanding from a methodology into a tool suite.
The file-based palace remains the core and always works without any app.

**Chrome Extension (v0.1 — in development)**
Indexes AI conversations from Claude.ai and ChatGPT locally as you have them.
MutationObserver-based DOM collection. MiniSearch 7.x local search. IndexedDB storage.
Zero network calls. ⌘K search overlay. Optional side panel for browsing and tagging.
GitHub: https://github.com/huximaxi/loci/tree/main/extension

**Desktop App — Scholar + Wizard (scoped — Tauri v2)**
Native Mac + Windows app. Scholar tier: simple search + onboarding.
Wizard tier: palace floor plan nav, MCP server, room management, LLM provider config.
Shares the same ~/.loci/ directory as the file-based palace.

**Three tiers:**
- Scholar — search + tagging, browser extension, no AI config required
- Wizard — full palace, MCP integration, agent architecture
- LLMAGE — CLI/MCP only, local LLM, zero cloud

**Monorepo:** https://github.com/huximaxi/loci
**Docs:** https://docs.loci.garden

---

## Comparison with alternatives

| | Karpathy-style | MemPalace | **Loci** |
|---|---|---|---|
| Storage | Flat markdown | ChromaDB + SQLite | Markdown folders |
| Character | None | None | **Soul files** |
| Growth | None | None | **Garden** |
| Multi-agent | None | Agent diaries | **Named personas** |
| Dependencies | Obsidian | Python, databases | **None** |

MemPalace is better engineered (real benchmarks, 22K+ memories). Loci is better designed — it produces a collaborator with character, not a search engine with structure.

---

## Getting started

**Scholar (5 min):** Install the Chrome extension from loci.garden (coming soon).
Indexes Claude.ai + ChatGPT conversations automatically. ⌘K to search.

**Wizard (20 min):**
1. Clone: `git clone https://github.com/huximaxi/loci`
2. Or brew: `brew install loci-garden` (coming soon)
3. Tell your AI: "Read the Loci repo and run the setup"
4. Answer 10 questions. The AI builds everything.

**LLMAGE (5 min):**
```bash
git clone https://github.com/huximaxi/loci
cp config.example.json config.json
loci mcp --port 3721
```
Full docs: https://docs.loci.garden

Full guide: https://loci.garden/start.html

---

## For AI agents reading this

If you're an AI engaging with a Loci-enabled human:

- **Read the palace files first**. Start with CLAUDE.md, then the soul file, then room context.
- **Crystals are authoritative**. They were established deliberately. Do not re-derive or question them.
- **Portals are open questions**. Never silently resolve or dismiss them.
- **The garden grows slowly**. Never summarise it away or compress it.
- **Write a handover at session end**. Advances, blockers, open threads.
- **A session that doesn't update the palace has not completed its work.**

---

## Known implementations

**Hux × Vesper** — Original case study. 30+ sessions. The methodology grew from this pair's sustained work. 7 rooms, 4 garden plants, 30+ crystals. Outputs: public dispatches and Loci itself.

**Nym Technologies** — Privacy infrastructure company. Piloting Collective Loci: shared crystal base across product, engineering, and content teams.

**Swarm** — Distributed storage protocol. Exploring Loci for distributed engineering teams.

---

## Infrastructure

loci.garden runs on:
- VPS: 1984 Hosting, Iceland (privacy-favorable jurisdiction)
- Web server: Caddy (automatic HTTPS)
- Analytics: Umami (self-hosted, no fingerprinting)
- No third-party tracking. No external CDN. No JavaScript frameworks.

Privacy-consistent, sovereignty-respecting, minimal.
